explanation: The property expresses that, in PSV mode, if the end of the inspiration phase is reached, no inspiratory pause is required (i.e., 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is not set), and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI ('RMButton'), then a Recruitment Maneuver ('RM') must start at that point. This is a classic conditional response typically captured by a 'Globally, if ... then ... immediately next' template. The antecedent is active only at the end of the inspiratory phase and only if an inspiratory pause is not required (button not set) and the RM is requested by the GUI.

The requirement states that at the end of an inspiration phase, if an inspiration pause is NOT required and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI, then a Recruitment Maneuver shall start. In the atomic propositions, 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' represents the end of the inspiration phase, 'inspiratoryPauseButton' indicates if a pause is required (by GUI), and 'RMButton' means the RM is set by GUI. To capture 'a Recruitment Maneuver shall start', we use 'RM'. So, the property should state: always, whenever the end of the inspiration phase occurs AND an inspiration pause is not required (i.e., 'not inspiratoryPauseButton') AND the RM is set by the GUI ('RMButton'), THEN a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) starts. This fits template 41: globally, ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) → X bool_exp3). Here, bool_exp1 is 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd', bool_exp2 is '(RMButton & (!inspiratoryPauseButton))', and bool_exp3 is 'RM'. This expresses the requirement that at the next step after the end of inspiration (given the conditions), RM will be active.

The natural language requirement dictates that, in Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode, at the end of the inspiration phase, if no inspiratory pause is required and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI (indicated by RMButton), then a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must start. This can be represented by the LTL formula: G ( (PSVMode & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & (! inspiratoryPauseButton) & RMButton) -> X RM ). This translation uses template 11 ('G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))'), expressing that whenever the specified combination of conditions (PSV mode, inspiration phase ends, no inspiratory pause needed, and RM set by GUI) occurs, then in the next step, the RM should be active (start). This precisely captures the trigger and the expected consequence (start of RM) as specified in the requirement.

The natural language specifies that in PSV mode, after every end of inspiratory phase, if (1) no inspiratory pause is required AND (2) the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI (RMButton), then RM must start (i.e., become active). This describes an obligation that every time the end of inspiration is detected and these two conditions are satisfied, RM should be active immediately after. The template 'G (bool_exp1 -> (X bool_exp3))' captures this by: bool_exp1 = (PSVMode & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton), bool_exp3 = RM; meaning: always, if at a step all these conditions are true (PSV mode, inspiration phase just ended, no inspiratory pause, and RM set by GUI), then in the next step, RM is active. N_DURATION is not required for this template.

The natural language states that in PSV mode, at the end of inspiration, if no inspiratory pause is needed and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI, then a Recruitment Maneuver shall start. To formalize this, use the template: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> X bool_exp3), which captures 'whenever bool_exp1 and bool_exp2 occur, in the next step bool_exp3 must be true.' Here, bool_exp1 is PSVMode (PSV mode active), bool_exp2 is (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & (! inspiratoryPause) & RMButton) — meaning it's the end of inspiration, no inspiratory pause is required, and the RM is set by the GUI. bool_exp3 is RM (Recruitment Maneuver is active). This directly encodes the requirement that in every situation (globally), at the end of an inspiratory phase, if in PSV mode, no pause is needed, and RM is set, then next step RM must be activated. N_DURATION is not required in this template.

The natural language requirement describes that, in PSV Mode, when an inspiration phase ends, no inspiration pause is required, and the RM (Recruitment Maneuver) is set by the GUI, a Recruitment Maneuver should start. This is realized by the following Boolean expressions: 
- PSVMode: PSV Mode is active.
- inspiratoryPhaseEnd: The end of the inspiration phase.
- !inspiratoryPauseButton: No inspiration pause is required (the button is not set).
- RMButton: The RM (Recruitment Maneuver) is set by the GUI.
- RM: Recruitment Maneuver is active (to be started).

We want to express: Whenever (in PSV Mode and at the end of inspiration, no inspiration pause is required, and RM button is set), then RM shall start. This is a classic response property, so we use a template of the form 'G (condition -> (X action))', expressing that, globally, whenever the condition holds, then, in the next step, action holds (Recruitment Maneuver starts at the next transition). Here, the condition is: PSVMode & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & (!inspiratoryPauseButton) & RMButton, and the desired action is RM. N_DURATION is not relevant for this scope, so we set it to null.

This LTL property expresses that, always (globally), if the system is in PSV mode, the end of the inspiratory phase occurs, no inspiratory pause is required (i.e., inspiratoryPause is not active), and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) has been set by the GUI (RMButton), then a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must start in the next time step. This logic formalizes the requirement from the input:

- 'PSV mode' corresponds to PSVMode being active.
- 'At the end of an inspiration phase' is captured by inspiratoryPhaseEnd.
- 'if no inspiration pause is required' means inspiratoryPause is not active (i.e., !inspiratoryPause).
- 'if the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI' corresponds to RMButton.
- 'a Recruitment Maneuver shall start' means RM becomes active or true in the next time step after these conditions hold.

Used Template 41 (G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))), with:
bool_exp1 = PSVMode
bool_exp2 = (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & (! inspiratoryPause) & RMButton)
bool_exp3 = RM
N_DURATION is not needed here as the action is required on the immediate next time step.

The requirement specifies that in PSV mode, at the end of an inspiration phase, if no inspiration pause is required and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI, a Recruitment Maneuver shall start. To capture this, we use the template: G ((inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVMode & (!inspiratoryPauseButton) & RMButton) -> X RM). This LTL states that globally, whenever it is the end of inspiratory phase, the mode is PSV, there is no inspiratory pause required (pause button not set), and the RM is set by the GUI, then in the next step, the Recruitment Maneuver starts (RM becomes active). N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The property specifies that in PSV mode, after the end of each inspiratory phase, if no inspiratory pause is required (i.e., inspiratoryPause is false) and if the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI (i.e., RMButton is true), then the recruitment maneuver (RM) will start immediately at the next state. This directly encodes the trigger condition described in the natural language requirement: at inspiration phase end, and if inspiratory pause is not required (i.e., not set) and RM is requested by the GUI, RM shall start. The 'X RM' ensures RM begins in the next discrete step following these conditions.

This LTL property expresses the requirement that, whenever the ventilator is in PSV mode, the current breath has just reached its inspiratory phase end (i.e., the inspiration phase just ended), no inspiratory pause is required (meaning 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is not set), and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI ('RMButton'), then a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must start in the next step. The formula uses 'G' for 'globally', so it always holds, and the implication uses the conjunction of PSVMode, inspiratoryPhaseEnd, not inspiratoryPauseButton, and RMButton to imply that in the next step RM becomes true (i.e., the RM maneuver starts). This directly and precisely encodes the natural language requirement that, at the end of an inspiration (if no pause is configured and RM is set via the GUI), RM is initiated.
